article_title: Fervo Energy Files for Nasdaq IPO Under Ticker FRVO, Cracking Open the Climate Tech Public Market for Enhanced Geothermal
summary: Substantively excellent Fervo IPO piece — most claims verify verbatim — but one factual error: 'climate tech VC fundraising in 2026 reached $6.5 billion' should be 2025 per TechCrunch ('last year'). Plus minor X-Energy framing nit ('first hour' vs 'first trading day').

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)
Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)
Wrong year: 'climate tech VC fundraising in 2026 reached $6.5 billion — matching 2021 levels'
findings.details: TechCrunch (article dated April 25, 2026) says 'Venture capital and growth funds raised about $6.5 billion last year' — 'last year' = 2025, not 2026. The $6.5B figure and 2021-parity framing are correct, but the year attribution should read '2025' not '2026'. Note: the $421M project-financing fact is also fully covered in InnovationMap (source-3), so the bot-blocked source-6 is not strictly needed.

findings.message: Minor: X-Energy '25% on its first trading day' vs TechCrunch's actual 'first hour of trading'
findings.details: Article: 'whose stock gained 25% on its first trading day'. TechCrunch: 'with the stock popping 25% in its first hour of trading'. The figure is right; tighten the time window.

content_preview.title: Fervo Energy Files for Nasdaq IPO Under Ticker FRVO, Cracking Open the Climate Tech Public Market for Enhanced Geothermal
content_preview.summary: Fervo Energy, the Houston enhanced geothermal developer behind Utah's Cape Station, filed an S-1 with the SEC on April 17 to list on the Nasdaq as FRVO, with banks led by J.P. Morgan and a private valuation around $3 billion.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Fervo Energy, the Houston-based enhanced geothermal developer that pioneered the application of horizontal drilling and fiber-optic sensing to subsurface heat extraction, has filed for an initial public offering on the Nasdaq. The company [publicly announced](https://fervoenergy.com/fervo-energy-publicly-announces-filing-of-registration-statement-for-proposed-initial-public-offering/) on April 17 that it had submitted a Form S-1 registration statement to the U.S. Securities and Exch...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically
Tighten X-Energy 'first trading day' → 'first hour of trading'.

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong Energy/Business News piece (~1018 words, in News range). Clear structure (Overview / What We Know / Why It Matters Now / What We Don't Know / Outlook). Tone analytical. Internal cross-link to Fervo-Turboden 4/8 article valid.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read 6 of 7 local snapshots (the 7th $421M financing source returned 403, but the $421M fact is also confirmed in InnovationMap source-3). Verified verbatim: April 17 S-1 filing / FRVO / Class A / Nasdaq / TBD shares-and-price / J.P. Morgan + BofA + RBC + Barclays leads / 8-bank additional bookrunner list (Fervo + GlobeNewswire); $3B PitchBook valuation / X-Energy $1B IPO / 25% first-hour pop (TechCrunch); $1.12B raised / 12 rounds / $462M Series E in December / $421M project financing / $70.5M 2025 net loss / Latimer 'tech companies compete for megawatts' quote (InnovationMap); 320 MW SCE PPA / two 15-year contracts / 350,000 homes equivalent (SCE PPA press release). Two issues: '2026' year attribution on $6.5B VC figure (TechCrunch says 'last year' = 2025); X-Energy 'first trading day' should be 'first hour of trading'.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All material facts verify, but one specific year is wrong (2026 → 2025) and one minor wording drift on the X-Energy time-window. Otherwise rigorous.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, analytical. Hedged appropriately on what the S-1 doesn't yet disclose (price, shares, timing).
editor_notes.originality: New topic — distinct from yesterday's Huaneng wind farm. Builds on Fervo-Turboden article via internal link.
concerns: One year-attribution error and one wording drift. Otherwise clean.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent IPO coverage with rich attribution. Two specific fixes and ready for re-review.
